steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The table shows tribal economic activity has the fewest employees (51,674) but $7.3 billion in revenue. The question asks which claim is supported by the data, so compare figures across all columns.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — tribal activity ranks highest in total contribution: Retail ($10.7B) exceeds tribal ($7.3B). ✗
  • Choice B — tribal activity employs the most workers: Tribal has the fewest employees at 51,674. ✗
  • Choice C — tribal activity generates the highest revenue per worker: $7.3B ÷ 51,674 = ~$141,510 per employee, well above retail (~$59,924) and accommodation/food services (~$34,861). ✓
  • Choice D — tribal employment equals 150,373: The table shows 51,674, not 150,373. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
